messages.content:
All federal buildings must have at least 3 working fire detectors.
Fire detectors must be located near entrances, exits, and any cooking facilities.
Fire detectors must be replaced after 5 years.
All fire detectors in a building should be replaced at the same time.
Based on the above information, which of the following statements MUST be true?
radio_button_unchecked
Fire extinguishers should be kept in the same location as fire detectors.
radio_button_unchecked
If the fire detectors in a building are 1, 3, and 6 years old, all of them should be replaced.
radio_button_unchecked
Federal buildings without cooking facilities only need 2 fire detectors.
radio_button_unchecked
If a federal building has 4 fire detectors, there must be 2 cooking facilities in the building.
radio_button_unchecked
A federal building with a cooking facility needs exactly 3 fire detectors.
If a federal building has a cooking facility, it must have at least 3 fire detectors.

All federal buildings must have at least 3 working fire detectors.
Fire detectors must be located near entrances, exits, and any cooking facilities.
Fire detectors must be replaced after 5 years.
All fire detectors in a building should be replaced at the same time.
Based on the above information, which of the following statements MUST be true?

The following statement MUST be true:

- If a federal building has a cooking facility, it needs exactly 3 fire detectors.

All clerks submit activity logs every Friday.
Erik is a clerk.
Alice submits one activity log each week - sometimes on Thursday and sometimes on Friday.

Based on the above statements, is the following conclusion true, false, or is there not enough information to determine?
Alice and Erik never submit activity logs on the same day of the week.

There is not enough information to determine whether the conclusion is true, false, or if there is not enough information to determine. The statement provided does not specify the specific days that Erik submits activity logs.

The American Tax Agency has eight regional customer care centers around the country (Center A, Center B, Center C, Center D, Center E, Center F, Center G, and Center H). During tax season, the following information applies to customer care hours at the centers:

• Half of the centers have extended hours.

• Centers A and G have the same hours.

• Centers B, C, and E have the same hours.

• Center F has extended hours.

• Center D does not have extended hours.

• Center D and E have the same hours.

• Center F and A have the same hours.



 Which of these Centers has extended customer care hours during tax season?

Based on the given information, the Centers with extended customer care hours during tax season are:

- Center F
- Center A

The American Tax Agency has eight regional customer care centers around the country (Center A, Center B, Center C, Center D, Center E, Center F, Center G, and Center H). During tax season, the following information applies to customer care hours at the centers:

• Half of the centers have extended hours.

• Centers A and G have the same hours.

• Centers B, C, and E have the same hours.

• Center F has extended hours.

• Center D does not have extended hours.

• Center D and E have the same hours.

• Center F and A have the same hours.



 Which of these Centers has extended customer care hours during tax season?

radio_button_unchecked
B
radio_button_checked
C
radio_button_unchecked
D
radio_button_unchecked
E
radio_button_unchecked
H

Based on the given information, the Centers with extended customer care hours during tax season are:

- Center C
- Center F

Schools should offer students advanced science, technology, engineering, and math (STEM) coursework. This will ensure that our students are prepared for well-paying jobs and that the United States remains competitive in the global economy of the future.
The argument above depends upon which of the following assumptions?
radio_button_unchecked
The United States is no longer competitive in the global economy.
radio_button_unchecked
Students often need encouragement to take coursework in science, technology, engineering, and math.
radio_button_unchecked
Jobs in the humanities often do not pay well.
radio_button_unchecked
Many schools already offer advanced STEM coursework, but it is not very effective.
radio_button_unchecked
Jobs in science, technology, engineering, and math offer high salaries.

The assumption that the argument above depends upon is:

- Jobs in science, technology, engineering, and math offer high salaries.

Bruce is in charge of planning a reception and dinner for six Division Directors. He is selecting a two-hour reservation time for the room, and the one-hour dinner will take place during that two-hour reservation. The following rules apply:

All six Directors must be able to attend the full dinner hour.
The dinner must occur between 5:00 p.m. and 9:00 p.m., and start on the hour.
The room must be reserved for two hours, and the reservation can start at any point in the hour.
Andrew and Felicity can attend at any time.
The earliest Ben can arrive is 5:30 p.m.
Candice must leave promptly at 8:30 p.m.
Denise can only attend between 6:00 p.m. and 8:00 p.m.
The earliest Frank can arrive is 6:45 p.m.
Which of the following reservation times should Bruce select?
radio_button_unchecked
4:00 p.m. to 6:00 p.m.
radio_button_unchecked
5:00 p.m. to 7:00 p.m.
radio_button_unchecked
5:30 p.m. to 7:30 p.m.
radio_button_unchecked
6:30 p.m. to 8:30 p.m.
radio_button_unchecked
8:00 p.m. to 10:00 p.m.

Bruce should select the following reservation time:

- 5:30 p.m. to 7:30 p.m.

Next week all employees will receive training on how to use the new accounting system.
New employees will also receive training on how to submit expense reports next week.
Based on the information above, which of these statements MUST be true?
radio_button_unchecked
Experienced employees already know how to submit expense reports.
radio_button_unchecked
New employees will receive two types of training next week.
radio_button_unchecked
New employees will receive the accounting system training before the expense report training.
radio_button_unchecked
Experienced employees will attend a different training session while the new employees are attending expense report training.
radio_button_unchecked
New employees are those employees who have been in their jobs for less than 6 months.

Based on the information provided, the statement that MUST be true is:

- New employees will receive two types of training next week.

Research suggests that workplace effectiveness improves with lower stress levels. The company of A & B Associates should improve workplace effectiveness by instituting mandatory yoga classes each morning before the workday starts.



Which of the following is an assumption required by this argument?

radio_button_unchecked
The mandatory yoga classes will lower the stress levels of employees at A & B Associates.
radio_button_unchecked
Yoga classes are better at lowering stress levels than are nutrition classes.
radio_button_unchecked
Many employees dislike mandatory yoga classes and consider the decision to participate in yoga to be a personal decision.
radio_button_unchecked
Mandatory yoga classes before work are more effective than yoga classes taken after work.
radio_button_unchecked
Employees who practice yoga regularly sleep better than those who do not.

The assumption required by this argument is:

- The mandatory yoga classes will lower the stress levels of employees at A & B Associates.

For a new project, staff members will work together in teams of two to review documents. Each team of two must include at least one staff member with prior document review experience. In addition, team members must be in the same time zone to ensure their work schedules are similar. The table below indicates whether each team member has prior experience and the time zone in which he or she works.

Staff Member	Prior Document Review Experience?	Time Zone
Teresa	Yes 	Eastern
Bart 	Yes 	Central
Kerry 	No  	Central
Alexis 	No 	Eastern
Drew 	Yes 	Central
Serena 	No 	Eastern
Luis 	Yes 	Eastern
Martin	No 	Central
Michelle 	Yes 	Central
Peter 	Yes 	Central
 

 

Which of the following staff members could work on a team with either Michelle or Kerry given the conditions described above?

radio_button_unchecked
Martin
radio_button_unchecked
Serena
radio_button_unchecked
Alexis
radio_button_unchecked
Bart
radio_button_unchecked
Luis

The staff member who could work on a team with either Michelle or Kerry given the conditions described above is:

- Bart

No files in Folder A are PowerPoint presentations.

All of Robert’s files are in Folder A.

Based on the above information, which of these statements MUST be true?

radio_button_unchecked
Some PowerPoint presentations are Robert's files.
radio_button_unchecked
All of Robert's files are PowerPoint presentations.
radio_button_unchecked
All files that are not Robert's are PowerPoint presentations.
radio_button_unchecked
All PowerPoint presentations are not Robert's files.
radio_button_unchecked
Some of Robert's files are PowerPoint presentations.

The statement that MUST be true based on the given information is:

- Some of Robert's files are not PowerPoint presentations.

Hard economic times often have a large impact on food banks. At the same time that the need for food banks increases, the rate of donations decreases because community members are less able to give. Therefore, our office charity project this year should be a food drive for our local food bank.
Which of the following statements, if true, would most strengthen the above argument?
radio_button_unchecked
Local economic conditions have been very poor over the past year.
radio_button_unchecked
The local humane society was recently given a very large cash donation.
radio_button_unchecked
Many food banks encourage cash donations.
radio_button_unchecked
The office held a food drive last year.
radio_button_unchecked
Experts recommend that a food drive should last at least two weeks.

The statement that would most strengthen the above argument is:

- Local economic conditions have been very poor over the past year.

Four items (A, B, C, and D) need to be shipped in exactly two packages today. The following restrictions apply:

Item B cannot be shipped by itself.
Item A cannot be shipped with item D.
Based on the above information, which of the following is an acceptable set of contents for one of the packages?
radio_button_unchecked
C
radio_button_unchecked
B and C
radio_button_unchecked
A and D
radio_button_unchecked
B and D
radio_button_unchecked
A, C, and D

Based on the given restrictions, an acceptable set of contents for one of the packages would be:

- A and D

All federal law enforcement officers are trained at facility X.

No federal security officers are trained at facility X.



Based on the above information, which of these statements MUST be true?

radio_button_unchecked
All federal law enforcement officers are not federal security officers.
radio_button_unchecked
All federal employees who are not security officers are law enforcement officers.
radio_button_unchecked
Some federal security officers are federal law enforcement officers.
radio_button_unchecked
Some federal law enforcement officers are federal security officers.
radio_button_unchecked
All federal employees who are not law enforcement officers are security officers.

Based on the given information, the statement that MUST be true is:

- All federal employees who are not security officers are law enforcement officers.

Five employees are scheduled to retire during the next fiscal year: Andre, Blair, Corey, Daniel, and Elizabeth.

• Elizabeth will retire before Corey.

• Corey will retire after Blair.

• Andre will retire before Corey but after Daniel.



If these people are listed in order of their retirement date from first to last, who will be the last to retire?

radio_button_unchecked
Andre
radio_button_unchecked
Blair
radio_button_unchecked
Corey
radio_button_unchecked
Daniel
radio_button_unchecked
Elizabeth

Based on the given information, the order of the employees' retirement dates from first to last will be:

- Daniel, Andre, Elizabeth, Blair, Corey

Therefore, Corey will be the last to retire.

This agency should spend additional funds on providing access to online training webinars. Employees should be able to develop themselves at their own convenience.



Which of the following statements, if true, would most seriously WEAKEN the above argument?

radio_button_unchecked
Employees who attend formal classroom training tend to learn more than those who take online training.
radio_button_unchecked
Multiple employees have requested additional training on a variety of topics over the last few years.
radio_button_unchecked
Employees with high workloads will build time into their schedules for online webinars.
radio_button_unchecked
Individual development, including training, is critical to the success of any organization.
radio_button_unchecked
Employees have stated that they will take advantage of any webinars made available to them.

The statement that would most seriously WEAKEN the above argument is:

- Employees who attend formal classroom training tend to learn more than those who take online training.

All members of Jeffrey’s team must attend the staff meeting.

Allison and Cathy are on the same team.

Cathy does not attend the staff meeting.



Based on the above statements, is the following conclusion true, or false, or is there not enough information to determine?

Allison is not a member of Jeffrey’s team.

radio_button_unchecked
True
radio_button_unchecked
False
radio_button_unchecked
Not Enough Information
The conclusion "Allison is not a member of Jeffrey's team" is false. The given statements do not provide enough information to determine whether Allison is a member of Jeffrey's team or not.

A manager has six customer service representatives (Joseph, Juliet, Jane, Ryan, Brian, and Pam) with varying levels of experience. We know that:

• Joseph has more customer service experience than Juliet.

• Juliet has less customer service experience than Jane.

• Juliet has more customer service experience than Ryan and Brian.

• Jane has less customer service experience than Pam.

 

Based on the above information, who has the LEAST customer service experience? 

radio_button_unchecked
Ryan
radio_button_unchecked
Joseph
radio_button_unchecked
Juliet
radio_button_unchecked
Jane
radio_button_unchecked
Pam

Based on the given information, the person with the LEAST customer service experience is:

- Juliet

Occupational Reasoning Assessment
The southern end of the parking lot will be snow plowed this afternoon so we should all move our cars to the northern end of the lot.
The argument above depends on which of the following assumptions?
radio_button_unchecked
The southern end of the lot is closer to the building entrance.
radio_button_unchecked
The northern end of the lot will be plowed next.
radio_button_unchecked
There are more spaces available on the southern end of the lot than the northern end of the lot.
radio_button_unchecked
Some cars are currently parked on the southern end of the lot.
radio_button_unchecked
You will receive a parking ticket if your car remains in the southern end of the lot.

The assumption that the argument above depends on is:

- Some cars are currently parked on the southern end of the lot.

Some reference books are stored in Room 10.
All newsletters must be stored in Room 12.
Julie stores books in Room 10.
Some newsletters are more than 5 years old.
All reference books belong to the research department.
Based on the information above, which of the following statements CANNOT be true?
radio_button_unchecked
Some reference books are stored in Room 12.
radio_button_unchecked
Julie stores newsletters.
radio_button_unchecked
All books stored by Julie belong to the research department.
radio_button_unchecked
All books stored in Room 10 belong to the research department.
radio_button_unchecked
All materials stored in Room 12 are less than 5 years old.

The statement that CANNOT be true based on the given information is:

- All materials stored in Room 12 are less than 5 years old.

This statement contradicts the information that some newsletters are more than 5 years old.

rewrite more personable : 
Thank you for keeping me updated on the status of my application and for taking the time to review my experience. Although I am disappointed, I appreciate your personalized email and wish you the best of luck on your search. I remain genuinely interested in joining the City of San Diego again and contributing to its success. If there are any future opportunities that align with my skills and experience, I would be grateful for your consideration.

Dear [Name],

I wanted to express my gratitude for the updates on my application and for the thoughtful review of my experience. While I'm disappointed with the outcome, I truly appreciate the personalized email. I want to extend my best wishes to you as you continue your search.

I wanted to reiterate my genuine interest in joining the City of San Diego again and being a part of its success. If there are any future opportunities that align with my skills and experience, I would be immensely grateful if you would consider me.

Thank you once again for your time and consideration.

Warm regards,
[Your Name]
